rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page. No monetary amounts visible; treated as a header.

rows.description: Computus Dni. Rectoris J. L. Richards
rows.notes: Page title line directly beneath year. Text legible; no amounts present.
Date line beneath title. No monetary values. Handwriting clear.
Continuation of heading text. No amounts visible.
Section heading for arrears/receipts. Legible script; no amounts.
Sub-header for this group of entries. Clear and unambiguous.

Number at extreme right reads as '60' when columns are seen together; using row 32 alignment this splits to 6 shillings and 0 pence. Vertical alignment with reference row (row 32) is consistent. Writing is clear.

Rightmost digits read '10'. Using the column boundaries projected from row 32, this most plausibly splits to 10 shillings and 0 pence. There is some ambiguity whether this could be '1 shilling 0 pence' vs '10 shillings', but the digit occupies the shilling column width consistent with 10s.
Clear three-part amount visible as '1 7 6' (1 pound, 7 shillings, 6 pence). Alignment with row 32 vertical guides is unambiguous.
Amount reads clearly as '9 6 8' (9 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence). Column positions align with row 32 reference; digits are distinct.
Amount appears as '6 13 4' (6 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence). Shillings=13 is within valid range; alignment clear using row 32.
Amount read as '1 13 4' (1 pound, 13 shillings, 4 pence). Clear three-part number; columns consistent with the alignment reference.
Amount reads '6 13 4' (6 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence). Clear and aligns with the established column boundaries.

Amount reads approximately as '1 19 1½' when aligned to row 32 guides: 1 pound, 19 shillings, 1 pence and a half (interpreted as 0.5). Fraction is small but visible; chosen reading consistent with page-specific guidance calling out fractional pence here.

Sub-item under previous entry (indicated by dashes). Rightmost grouping reads '17 6' which, per alignment, is 17 shillings 6 pence. Text partially abbreviated; amount aligns with established column boundaries.

Sub-item reading appears as '7 1' in the shillings/pence area. Ink is a little faint on the pence digit; interpreted as 7 shillings and 1 pence using alignment from row 32 but alternate readings (e.g., 7s 11d) are less likely but possible.
Amount reads '10' in the shilling/pence area; with alignment this is 10 shillings, 0 pence. Writing is clear for both digits.
Section header in the left description column. No monetary values on the same line.
Amount reads as '11 2 9' (11 pounds, 2 shillings, 9 pence). Clear triple-part number; columns aligned with reference row 32.

Rightmost group reads '28'. Using the column guide from row 32 this splits to 2 shillings and 8 pence rather than 28 shillings (which would be invalid). The reading is moderately confident but the compact digits make it somewhat ambiguous.
Amount reads '4 4' (4 shillings, 4 pence) with good alignment to the established column boundaries.
Rightmost marking appears as '11' or '11 1' when split by alignment. Interpreted as 11 shillings and 1 pence given the spacing; the pence digit is faint so the last digit has some uncertainty.
Section heading for variable rents. Clearly written; no amounts on this line.
Amount reads clearly as '84 0 6' (84 pounds, 0 shillings, 6 pence). Large pound figure aligns to the leftmost of the three currency columns (checked against row 32 guide); digits are distinct.
Amount reads '34 8 4' (34 pounds, 8 shillings, 4 pence). Clear three-part number with good alignment to the reference row.
Amount appears as '107 14 3' (107 pounds, 14 shillings, 3 pence). Pound column clearly contains three digits and rest align with the guides from row 32.

rows.description: Jth Newington Rectory 1 yr (to L Day 1838)
rows.notes: Amount read as '181 19 9' (181 pounds, 19 shillings, 9 pence). Very clear three-part amount; alignment consistent with row 32 reference.

Amount shows '2 14 8¼' when aligned with row 32 guides: 2 pounds, 14 shillings, 8 pence and a farthing (0.25). Fractional farthing mark is small but visible; moderate confidence due to the tiny fraction glyph.

Amount aligned and read as '239 7 5¾' (239 pounds, 7 shillings, 5 pence and three farthings (0.75)). Fractional mark is visible and consistent with the page-specific guidance calling out fractional values in these rows.

Amount reads as '2 10 10½' (2 pounds, 10 shillings, 10 pence and a half (0.5)). The halfpenny symbol is small but present; alignment confirmed with row 32. Some ink faintness on the fraction reduces confidence slightly.

Amount read as '15 13 3¾' (15 pounds, 13 shillings, 3 pence and three farthings (0.75)). Fraction mark visible; digits align well with row 32 guide. Some minor smudging near the fraction but reading is consistent.

Final total line used as the primary column alignment reference (as instructed). Read as 34 pounds, 7 shillings, 3 and three-quarter pence (0.75). Numerals are well aligned and relatively clear; fraction is visible and consistent with other fractional markings on the page.
